title: DFT math and bin frequency resolution
description: The DFT maps N time samples to N frequency bins spaced at sample_rate over N, fixing the achievable frequency resolution.
body: Use when reasoning about which frequencies an FFT can distinguish. Do not expect finer resolution from zero-padding alone, because padding interpolates the spectrum without adding real information. Actions: compute bin spacing as sample rate divided by transform length, verify a tone lands near its expected bin, and record the interpolation versus true-resolution distinction. Validation signals include a synthetic tone appearing within one bin of its known frequency and resolution scaling inversely with block length. Risk boundary: conflating zero-pad interpolation with true resolution misleads measurement claims, so flag any resolution assertion that ignores the underlying record length.
